checks.a_analysis: The output does not contain any fabricated paragraphs or sentences. All headings, body text, and structural markers such as 'This page left blank intentionally' correspond to content present in the source document.

The output contains no fabricated phone numbers, emails, addresses, or URLs. While a specific URL from the source was omitted during conversion (documented in C6), no false or incorrect contact information was generated.
All pages and major sections identified in the source PDF, including the title page, table of contents, and all content sections, are accounted for in the conversion output.

All tables present in the source PDF—including the multi-page project status table and the budget expense tables—were converted with the correct number of rows and data points. No rows are missing from the financial or status data.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: Two significant text omissions were identified. First, in the 'Potential Participants' section (page 8), the output fails to include the approximately 30 department names listed as sub-items under the eight main categories (e.g., VPN Administrator Access). This constitutes a loss of over 60% of the section's content, which is a MAJOR defect. Second, in the 'Introduction' section (page 5), the descriptive sub-bullets defining types of authentication (e.g., 'Something you know...', 'Something you have...') are missing, though this omission is less than 25% of the total section content.
checks.b_evidence_source: 1. VPN (Administrator Access) o Department of Information Technology o Department of Public Safety o Department of Revenue o Department of Human and Health Services o Department of Natural and Cultural Resources o Department of Administration o Department of Transportation

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: A minor content fidelity issue exists in the 'Legislative Request' section on page 5, where the reference URL was omitted. This results in a broken sentence: 'See for reference.' instead of 'See http://www.ncleg.net/Sessions/2015/Bills/House/PDF/H97v9.pdf for reference.' Additionally, the missing sub-bullets in the Introduction (noted in C5) serve as a second minor content omission that alters the level of detail available to the reader.

checks.a_analysis: I carefully compared the content of each section in the output with the source PDF. There are no instances of duplicated text blocks, paragraphs, or sections. Each element in the output corresponds to a unique portion of the source document.

The output maintains a logical heading hierarchy. H1 is used for the document title and main 'Contents' heading. H2 is consistently used for major sections (Legislative Request, Introduction, Background, etc.). H3 is used correctly for 'Goals' as a subsection of 'The MFA Project'. No levels are skipped, and the nesting reflects the source's organization.

The table structure for the MFA Project status on page 10 is corrupted. In the output's 'Plan' section, the first table (element 79) is structurally inconsistent: the first four rows contain 2 columns, while the fifth row contains 3 columns. This results in misaligned data and a broken table structure compared to the visual alignment in the source.
checks.b_evidence_source: The Project Status list on page 10 showing '5. Procurement Phase Pending Jan 2016' as a single entry area.
checks.b_evidence_output: The table with index 79 has rows with differing cell counts (e.g., Row 1 has 2 cells, Row 5 has 3 cells: '5. Procurement Phase', 'Pending', 'Jan 2016').

checks.a_analysis: The reading order in the output faithfully follows the top-to-bottom, page-by-page flow of the source document. The logical progression from the title page to the table of contents and through the subsequent report sections is preserved.

The list structure is significantly degraded. On page 8, under 'Potential Participants', the source contains a complex nested list (numbered items with multiple bullet points under each). The output (element 52) renders this as a flat list of 8 items, completely omitting all nested bullet points and their associated content. Similarly, the sub-bullets under 'Types of authentication include' on page 5 are missing from the list in element 27.
checks.b_evidence_source: 1. VPN (Administrator Access) o Department of Information Technology o Department of Public Safety ... [and so on for 8 numbered items with bullets]
checks.b_evidence_output: A flat list of 8 items containing only the numbered headings (e.g., 'VPN (Administrator Access)', 'Payment Card Industry Servers'). All indented bullet points are absent from the structure.
checks.b_evidence_location: Potential Participants section, page 8, element index 52

checks.a_analysis: A significant government URL present in the source is missing from the output. On page 5, the text 'See http://www.ncleg.net/Sessions/2015/Bills/House/PDF/H97v9.pdf for reference.' is split into two elements (19 and 21), and the link itself (index 20 in the logical sequence) is absent.
checks.b_evidence_output: The text around the link exists ('See' in element 19 and 'for reference.' in element 21), but the URL and link element are missing.
checks.b_evidence_location: Legislative Request section, page 5, between element 19 and 21

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output maintains the single-column visual organization of the source PDF. Content blocks (Legislative Request, Introduction, Background, etc.) follow the same logical flow. The inclusion of the navigation sidebar (TOC) is a standard accessibility feature and does not disrupt the primary content layout. The 'This page left blank intentionally' placeholders are preserved from the source's layout.

All tabular data is correctly rendered. The budget information from page 12 of the PDF is converted into clean, bordered HTML tables. Additionally, the project status lists from pages 10 and 11 have been appropriately formatted into tables (Table 1 and Table 6), which enhances readability without losing information or visual coherence.
The meaningful NC Information Technology logo from the cover page of the source PDF is present and rendered correctly in the HTML output.

There is no evidence of body content duplication. Each section (paragraphs and tables) appears exactly once in the rendered body of the HTML. The presence of headings in both the sidebar table of contents and the body is an intentional accessibility feature, not a duplication defect.
